mission.title: Install and Configure Kyverno (Policy Engine)
mission.description: Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that allows you to manage security, compliance, and governance through policy-as-code. It is particularly useful for teams looking to enforce policies consistently across their Kubernetes clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for a learning curve to define policies and potential performance impacts if overly complex policies are applied.

steps.title: Add the Kyverno Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kyverno Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno

```

> This is the official repository for Kyverno.

steps.title: Install Kyverno with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Kyverno in the 'kyverno' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kyverno

helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

```

> Create a namespace for Kyverno.
> Install Kyverno using the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the Kyverno installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kyverno pods are running and ready in the 'kyverno' namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> This command lists all pods in the kyverno namespace.

steps.title: Create an example policy
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Kyverno policy to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

```

> This policy enforces that all Pods must have an 'app' label.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

steps.title: Verify the policy is working
steps.description:
Check the status of the policy to ensure it is applied correctly.

```bash

kubectl get cpol require-labels -o yaml

```

> This command retrieves the details of the created policy.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kyverno
uninstall.description:
This step will remove Kyverno and all associated resources. Be aware that any policies defined will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno

kubectl delete namespace kyverno

```

> Uninstalls the Kyverno release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kyverno
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your policies and configurations. This step will upgrade Kyverno to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get cpol -n kyverno -o yaml > kyverno-policies-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Backup existing policies.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get cpol -n kyverno -o yaml > kyverno-policies-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'validation failure action must be one of enforce, audit, or dryrun'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The validationFailureAction specified in the policy is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the validationFailureAction is set to 'enforce', 'audit', or 'dryrun'.

troubleshooting.title: Kyverno pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kyverno pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kyverno' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Policy not enforcing as expected.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The policy may not match the resources being created.

**Fix:**
Review the policy's match rules to ensure they correctly target the intended resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to find apiVersion' when applying a policy.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The policy YAML may have an incorrect apiVersion.

**Fix:**
Ensure the apiVersion is set to 'kyverno.io/v1' for Kyverno policies.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new validation rules and improved error messages.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kyverno pods running with a status of 'Running' and 'READY' column showing 1/1 when you run 'kubectl get pods -n kyverno'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and have kubectl and helm installed.
